title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries · Stakeholder Case: Qualified Review
summary: The product depends on health, finance, education, civic, creative, or safety assumptions inferred from secondary summaries and incompatible licenses. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone qualified review increment in domain authority and compliance boundary dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review
steps.instruction:
theory case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Qualified Review contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review. For Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review, keep Qualified Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Qualified Review condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

hints: For Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Qualified Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

For Predict Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Adding a disclaimer removes the need for qualified review.

options.text: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Adding a disclaimer removes the need for qualified review. That claim does not survive the predict qualified review evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Bound Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review
steps.instruction:
theory case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Qualified Review application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review. For Bound Qualified Review · Theory Qualified Review, keep Qualified Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review. A defensible solution applies Qualified Review, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to domain authority and compliance boundary dossier.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Adding a disclaimer removes the need for qualified review. This fails the Qualified Review contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Qualified Review evidence · Theory Qualified Review

For Build Qualified Review evidence · Theory Qualified Review, keep Qualified Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Qualified Review model · Theory Qualified Review
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Qualified Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
